source: Crosswalk authored by Saaksh from the MSCI ESG Ratings Key Issue Framework (10 themes / 35 Key Issues across 3 pillars), the S&P Global Corporate Sustainability Assessment (CSA) which underpins the Dow Jones Sustainability Indices (DJSI), CDP's full corporate questionnaire, and the EcoVadis ratings methodology. Mapped at the BRSR principle level, the granularity at which these assessments actually score companies.
note: Indicative, a consultant aid to show which BRSR data feeds which assessment. It is not an official MSCI / S&P / CDP / EcoVadis mapping, and each scheme revises its questionnaire and criteria periodically (CDP restructures annually). Confirm against the current questionnaire before relying on it for a submission.

primary_sources.label: CDP, Full Corporate Questionnaire Overview (v1.0, released 20 April 2026)
EcoVadis, Ratings Methodology Overview and Principles

msci.name: MSCI ESG Ratings
msci.what: A risk-based rating (CCC to AAA) measuring a company's resilience to financially material ESG risks. Built from 35 Key Issues; 7–15 are scored per sub-industry.

djsi.name: S&P Global CSA / DJSI
djsi.what: A best-in-class score from the annual Corporate Sustainability Assessment (CSA); the top performers per industry enter the Dow Jones Sustainability Indices.

cdp.name: CDP
cdp.what: An annual environmental disclosure, scored A to D-. Climate change, forests and water security run through one integrated corporate questionnaire and are the scored issues; biodiversity, plastics and ocean are disclosed but unscored. CDP is environmental only, so it never reaches BRSR's workforce, human-rights, community or consumer principles.

ecovadis.name: EcoVadis
ecovadis.what: A supplier sustainability rating scored out of 100 (with medals), across four themes and 21 criteria. Every theme is scored on Policies (25%), Actions (40%) and Results (35%).

mappings.note: BRSR P1 anti-bribery, board oversight and penalty disclosures feed the Governance pillar that MSCI scores for every company. The board-oversight answer also feeds CDP's Governance module, and the anti-corruption policy is the whole of the EcoVadis Ethics theme.

LCA, recycled-input and EPR data support MSCI product-impact issues and the CSA Product Stewardship criterion. Packaging and EPR feed CDP's plastics disclosure; sustainable sourcing (P2-E2) is the EcoVadis Sustainable Procurement theme.
BRSR P3 safety, benefits and training metrics map almost one-to-one to the CSA Human Capital criteria and to four EcoVadis Labour criteria. CDP does not cover workforce topics.

Structured stakeholder identification and materiality underpin the CSA Materiality criterion that gates much of the assessment. CDP picks this up only where you engage the value chain on environmental issues; the EcoVadis overlap is narrow.

ecovadis_criteria: Child labour, forced labour & human trafficking
mappings.note: BRSR P5 human-rights due diligence and supplier audits feed MSCI supply-chain issues, the CSA Human Rights criterion, and both the Labour and Sustainable Procurement themes in EcoVadis. CDP does not cover human rights.

cdp_areas: Identification, assessment & management of dependencies, impacts, risks & opportunities
Disclosure of dependencies, impacts, risks & opportunities
mappings.note: The richest overlap by far: BRSR P6 energy, GHG, water and waste data drive MSCI's Climate/Natural-Capital themes, the CSA Environmental dimension, almost every scored CDP module, and the EcoVadis Environment theme. Collect P6 once and it feeds all four.

BRSR P7 trade-association and advocacy disclosures map to the CSA Policy Influence criterion, increasingly scrutinised for climate-policy alignment. CDP asks whether your policy engagement is consistent with your climate commitments.

msci_key_issues: Access to Finance / Health Care / Communications
mappings.note: CSR, local employment and community-impact data support MSCI social-opportunity issues and the CSA community criteria. Neither CDP nor EcoVadis scores community investment directly; only the local/MSME sourcing part reaches EcoVadis.

BRSR P9 data-privacy, product-safety and grievance disclosures feed MSCI Product-Liability issues and the CSA cyber & customer criteria. In EcoVadis, product safety sits under Environment and data privacy under Ethics. CDP does not cover consumer topics.
